title: PACELC — C vs A under partition

panels.title: Experiment 1 (EL): replica lag — versions behind primary
panels.description: Healthy, async link under write load. 'primary n' and 'replica n' are the absolute counter values (left axis) — they climb together and look nearly identical at full scale. 'versions behind' = primary_n − replica_n (RIGHT axis), the gap auto-scaled so the lag is visible: the replica can't replay in real time and trails by some versions. Pure latency cost, no partition — PACELC's Else-Latency corner.

panels.title: Experiment 2 (AP): replica diverges, both stay up
panels.description: Async + partition. The replication link is cut: the primary keeps committing (its line climbs) while the replica freezes at its last value — stale, but both nodes stay available (AP). On heal the replica snaps back up to the primary (eventual consistency).

panels.title: Experiment 3 (CP): writes block instead of diverging
panels.description: Synchronous + partition. Every commit must be acked by the standby, so cutting the link makes a write BLOCK (it does NOT error — it just hangs). 'write blocked' climbs while the commit is stuck, showing a write IS in flight; on heal it completes and 'write p99' records the full stalled duration.

panels.title: System state
panels.description: Context for the panels above. standby_connected: 1 while the standby is streaming. partitioned: 1 while the replication link is cut. sync_mode: 1 while synchronous_standby_names is set. Read each experiment against these windows.
